briefdescription.para: reset palette
texture and legend must be updated
uv-coordinates must be recomputed for the same values

detaileddescription.para: return struct of ImGui::Palette values are bits

briefdescription.para: Default setup.
Parameters for the Plane( MR::PlaneWidget& ... ) function.

memberdef.name: vector_getter

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, int *idx, std::function< const char *(int)> getter, int itemsCount)
memberdef.name: Combo

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, std::string &str, ImGuiInputTextFlags flags=0, ImGuiInputTextCallback callback=NULL, void *user_data=NULL)
memberdef.name: InputText

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, float *value, float speed=1.0f, float min=std::numeric_limits< float >::lowest(), float max=std::numeric_limits< float >::max(), const char *format="%.3f", ImGuiSliderFlags flags=0)
memberdef.name: DragFloatValid

detaileddescription.para: similar to ImGui::DragFloat but 1) value on output is forced to be in [min,max] range; 2) tooltip about Shift/Alt is shown when the item is active, and the valid range

briefdescription.para: similar to ImGui::DragFloatValid but use available line width range

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, float v[2], float v_speed=1.0f, float min=std::numeric_limits< float >::lowest(), float max=std::numeric_limits< float >::max(), const char *format="%.3f", ImGuiSliderFlags flags=0, const char *(*tooltips)[2]=nullptr)
memberdef.name: DragFloatValid2

detaileddescription.para: similar to ImGui::DragFloat2 - two drag-float controls in a row, but 1) returns information whether an item was deactivated; 2) calls DragFloatValid inside instead of DragFloat; 3) permits showing tooltip for each item

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, float v[3], float v_speed=1.0f, float min=std::numeric_limits< float >::lowest(), float max=std::numeric_limits< float >::max(), const char *format="%.3f", ImGuiSliderFlags flags=0, const char *(*tooltips)[3]=nullptr)
memberdef.name: DragFloatValid3

detaileddescription.para: similar to ImGui::DragFloat3 - three drag-float controls in a row, but 1) returns information whether an item was deactivated; 2) calls DragFloatValid inside instead of DragFloat; 3) permits showing tooltip for each item

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, int *value, float speed=1, int min=std::numeric_limits< int >::lowest(), int max=std::numeric_limits< int >::max(), const char *format="%d")
memberdef.name: DragIntValid

detaileddescription.para: similar to ImGui::DragInt but 1) value on output is forced to be in [min,max] range; 2) tooltip about Shift/Alt is shown when the item is active, and the valid range

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, int v[3], float speed=1, int min=std::numeric_limits< int >::lowest(), int max=std::numeric_limits< int >::max(), const char *format="%d", const char *(*tooltips)[3]=nullptr)
memberdef.name: DragIntValid3

detaileddescription.para: similar to ImGui::DragInt3 - three drag-int controls in a row, but 1) returns information whether an item was deactivated; 2) calls DragIntValid inside instead of DragInt; 3) permits showing tooltip for each item

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, int *value, int min, int max, int step=1, int step_fast=100, ImGuiInputTextFlags flags=0)
memberdef.name: InputIntValid

detaileddescription.para: similar to ImGui::InputInt but 1) value on output is forced to be in [min,max] range; 2) tooltip about valid range is shown when the item is active

briefdescription.para: draw check-box that takes initial value from Getter and then saves the final value in Setter

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *str_id, std::function< float(int idx)> values_getter, std::function< void(int idx)> tooltip, std::function< void(int idx)> on_click, int values_count, int values_offset=0, float scale_min=FLT_MAX, float scale_max=FLT_MAX, ImVec2 frame_size=ImVec2(0, 0), int selectedBarId=-1, int hoveredBarId=-1, const std::vector< HistogramGridLine > &gridIndexes={}, const std::vector< HistogramGridLine > &gridValues={})
memberdef.name: PlotCustomHistogram

detaileddescription.para:
draws a histogram
selectedBarId
(if not negative) the bar to highlight as selected
hoveredBarId
(if not negative) the bar to highlight as hovered

briefdescription.para: begin typical state plugin window

detaileddescription.para: returns the position of the window that will be located at the bottom of the viewport for a value pivot = ( 0.0f, 1.0f )

briefdescription.para: Calculate and return the height of the window title.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, ImGuiWindow *window, float width, const ImVec2 *position=nullptr)
memberdef.name: LoadSavedWindowPos

detaileddescription.para: Load saved window position, if possible

if can't load - get position (if setted) or default (upper-right viewport corner) see also SaveWindowPosition
label
window label
width
window width
position
(optional) preliminary window position
pair of the final position of the window and flag whether the position was loaded
Save window position
saved only if window exist see also LoadSavedWindowPos
Same as ImGui::Begin, but with loading and saving the window position
see also LoadSavedWindowPos and SaveWindowPosition

begin state plugin window with custom style. if you use this function, you must call EndCustomStatePlugin to close the plugin correctly. the flags ImGuiWindowFlags_NoScrollbar and ImGuiWindow_NoScrollingWithMouse are forced in the function.

briefdescription.para: end state plugin window with custom style
starts modal window with no animation for background

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, int *v, const MR::BitSet &bs, int step=1, int step_fast=100, ImGuiInputTextFlags flags=0)
memberdef.name: InputIntBitSet

briefdescription.para: same as ImGui::InputInt

detaileddescription.para:
Input int according valid from BitSet 
true if value was changed and valid 


if inputted invalid value, it will be changed to first upper valid value (or find_last)
value can't be changed if bs has no any valid value

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, int *value, float speed=1, int min=std::numeric_limits< int >::lowest(), int max=std::numeric_limits< int >::max(), const char *format="%d", ImGuiSliderFlags flags=ImGuiSliderFlags_None)
memberdef.name: DragInputInt

briefdescription.para: Combine of ImGui::DragInt and ImGui::InputInt.

detaileddescription.para:
Value can be changed by drag or "+"/"-" buttons
used new visual style

briefdescription.para: Draw text as link, calls callback on click.

detaileddescription.para: Draw text as link, colored with blue, calls callback on click

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *label, MR::Palette &palette, std::string &presetName, float width, bool *fixZero=nullptr, float speed=1.0f, float min=std::numeric_limits< float >::lowest(), float max=std::numeric_limits< float >::max())
memberdef.name: Palette

detaileddescription.para:
Helper palette widget, allows to change palette ranges and filter type can load and save palette preset.
presetName
stores the currently selected palette's preset name or empty string if the palette was edited by user
fixZero
if present shows checkbox to fix zero symmetrical palette
mask of changes, if it has PaletteChanges::Texture bit - object requires texture update, if it has PaletteChanges::Ranges uv coordinates should be recalculated and updated in object
Helper plane widget, allows to draw specified plain in the scene can import plane from the scene, draw it with mouse or adjust with controls planeWidget stores the plane widget params

briefdescription.para: draw image with Y-direction inversed up-down
get image coordinates under cursor considering Y-direction flipping
draw spinner in given place, radius with respect to scaling
draw big title with close cross (i.e. for settings modal popup )
draw exit button with close cross (i.e. for settings modal popup )
get exponential speed for this value
A crude conversion to grayscale. Good enough for our purposes.
return true if ImGui Multi Viewport enabled

detaileddescription.para: Extend ImGui by populating its namespace directly
Code snippets taken from there: https:/// eliasdaler.github.io/using-imgui-with-sfml-pt2/
